articles.summary: The Ministry of Ayush and the drug regulator are planning to amend Schedule K of the Drugs and Cosmetics Rules to allow allopathic wholesalers to stock and distribute homoeopathy medicines. The move leverages the robust distribution network of the $50 billion allopathic market to ensure affordable homoeopathy drugs are available in remote areas without requiring separate storage facilities.

articles.headline: Commentary: Systemic workplace stress needs structural change, not wellness apps
articles.summary: Amrita Puri argues that corporate wellness initiatives like yoga mats and meditation rooms are performative and fail to address the root causes of workplace distress: toxic management and long hours. With mental disorders potentially costing India $1.03 trillion by 2030, she calls for structural shifts like psychological safety and clearly defined KPIs to ensure long-term sustainability.

articles.headline: Regulator Acts Against Dangerous Illegal Fixed Dose Drug Combinations
articles.summary: India's drug regulator has launched a crackdown on unapproved Fixed Dose Combinations (FDCs) identified as serious health risks. Over 90 drugs, including treatments for diabetes and multivitamins, are under scrutiny. Manufacturers and distributors have been ordered to stop the sale of these unapproved products immediately.

articles.headline: Pharma and FMCG Firms Partner for Weight Loss Solutions
articles.summary: Companies like Nestle, Danone, and Dr. Reddy's are collaborating to offer specialized dietary products for people using weight-loss drugs like semaglutide. This 'dietary support' ecosystem includes protein supplements and fiber-rich foods designed to maintain muscle mass during treatment. The market for such support products is projected to reach ₹8,000 crore by 2030.

articles.headline: Over 50 percent of PHC officer posts vacant in Karnataka
articles.summary: More than half of the sanctioned primary healthcare officer (PHCO) positions in Karnataka remain vacant, severely impacting rural health delivery. Of the 4,994 sanctioned posts, only 2,300 are currently occupied. The shortage is most acute in Dakshina Kannada, where only 251 out of 444 positions are filled. Health department officials attribute the gap to long-standing recruitment delays.

articles.headline: India Sports Economy Doubled in Four Years to 2.13 Billion Dollars
articles.summary: According to a WPP Media report, India's sports industry grew 13.4% in 2025 to reach ₹18,864 crore. Cricket accounts for 89% of the total industry, with IPL team sponsorships crossing the ₹1,000 crore mark. While ad spends rose nearly 20%, emerging sports saw a 12.2% decline due to a lack of major multi-nation tournaments, though new sports like pickleball are gaining momentum.

articles.headline: RCB seeks to defend IPL title as Virat Kohli remains icon
articles.summary: Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru enter the 19th IPL season with Virat Kohli as their 'cultural architect.' Though led by Rajat Patidar, the team relies on Kohli's massive brand value and recent high-scoring form. RCB looks to maintain the winning spirit that secured their first title last year, despite impending ownership changes and external challenges at their home stadium.

articles.headline: India pacer Akash Deep ruled out of IPL with back injury
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders bowler Akash Deep will miss at least eight weeks due to a stress reaction in his lower back. The injury also makes him doubtful for India’s upcoming one-off Test against Afghanistan. KKR is already facing a pace crisis with injuries to Harshit Rana and the release of Mustafizur Rahman, leading the team to hold trials for experienced replacements like Akash Madhwal.

articles.headline: Manav Thakkar and Yashaswini Ghorpade win maiden national TT titles
articles.summary: PSPB paddlers Manav Thakkar and Yashaswini Ghorpade secured their first singles titles at the Senior National Table Tennis Championships in Indore. Thakkar dominated the men's final 4-1 against Jeet Chandra, while Ghorpade won a thrilling seven-game women's final against Syndrela Das. The victory marks a significant milestone for both athletes in the domestic circuit.

articles.headline: Danny Welbeck brace leads Brighton to victory over Liverpool
articles.summary: Brighton defeated Liverpool 2-1 as Danny Welbeck scored twice, damaging the Reds' chances for a Champions League spot. Welbeck became the top English scorer in the Premier League this season with his double. Despite a goal from Milos Kerkez, Arne Slot’s team suffered their 10th league defeat, raising questions about the manager's future as Brighton keeps its European hopes alive.

articles.headline: Indian shuttlers Tanvi Sharma and Isharani Baruah exit Orleans semi-finals
articles.summary: India's campaign at the Orleans Masters ended as Tanvi Sharma and Isharani Baruah lost their respective semi-final matches. 17-year-old Tanvi fell in straight games to former world champion Nozomi Okuhara. Isharani Baruah was defeated by Thailand's Pitchamon Opatniputh in a hard-fought match, bringing an end to Indian hopes in the $250,000 badminton tournament.

articles.headline: Japan defeats Australia 1-0 to win third Women's Asian Cup title
articles.summary: Japan claimed the Women's Asian Cup title after a 1-0 victory over hosts Australia in Sydney. Maika Hamano scored the only goal of the match in the 17th minute before a record crowd of over 74,000 spectators. This marks Japan's third continental title in the last four editions, while Australia’s Matildas failed to capitalize on home advantage in the final.

articles.headline: Abhay Singh reaches JSW Indian Open squash final in Mumbai
articles.summary: World No.26 Abhay Singh advanced to the final of the JSW Indian Open after defeating Malaysia's Ameeshenraj Chandaran 3-1. Singh, the current India No.1, will face fellow Indian Veer Chotrani in the final. Veteran Saurav Ghosal praised Abhay as a great example of a player maximizing his potential through hard work and training under English world No.1 James Willstrop.

articles.headline: Rajesh Ramesh and Vithya Ramraj win 400m gold in Thiruvananthapuram
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu athletes Rajesh Ramesh and Vithya Ramraj clinched gold medals in the 400m events at the season-opening Indian Open. Ramesh clocked a personal best of 45.26 seconds to win the men's Race A, while Ramraj won the women's category in 53.31 seconds. The event marks a strong start for the state's athletes in the national athletics calendar.

articles.headline: Punjab FC secures 3-1 win over Kerala Blasters in ISL
articles.summary: Punjab FC defeated Kerala Blasters 3-1 in an Indian Super League match at Kochi. Goals from Samir Zeljkovic, Bede Osuji, and Dani Ramirez secured the three points for the visitors. Fallou Ndiaye scored a consolation goal for the Blasters. With this win, Punjab FC moved up to sixth place in the league standings, while Kerala Blasters remain in 13th position.

articles.headline: Cricket dominates 89% of India's $2 billion sports sponsorship market
articles.summary: India's sports economy crossed $2 billion in 2025, growing 13.4% to ₹18,864 crore, despite the exit of real-money gaming advertisers. Cricket remains the dominant force, contributing 89% of the industry size. Conversely, sponsorship for emerging sports declined 12.2% to ₹1,193 crore, largely due to the absence of the Indian Super League (ISL) football season in the reporting period.

articles.headline: India prepares for T20 World Cup Super 8 clash against Afghanistan
articles.summary: The Indian cricket team will face Afghanistan in Barbados for their first Super 8 match of the T20 World Cup. Following an unbeaten group stage run, the team is focusing on adjusting to Caribbean pitches. Captain Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli’s opening partnership remains a key talking point as the tournament enters its knockout phase.

articles.headline: Barcelona Remains Top of La Liga Following Araujo Goal
articles.summary: Ronald Araujo’s late goal secured a 1-0 victory for Barcelona against Rayo Vallecano, keeping the team seven points clear at the top of La Liga. Real Madrid remains in second place after a draw in the Madrid derby. Barcelona's consistent performance has made them firm favorites to win the league title as the season enters its final stages.

articles.headline: LeBron James Approaches Historic NBA Scoring and Minutes Milestones
articles.summary: NBA legend LeBron James is nearing several historic records, including most regular-season games won and total field goals attempted. He has already surpassed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's points record and is close to logging the most minutes played in NBA history. James’ longevity and consistency continue to redefine greatness in professional basketball.

articles.headline: RCB returns home to Bengaluru with hopes for IPL title
articles.summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) have returned to their home ground after a series of away matches, carrying both scars and hope for the current IPL season. Despite past losses and current squad weaknesses, the team remains a fan favorite. Management is focusing on stabilizing the middle order and improving bowling depth to secure a spot in the playoffs and aim for the elusive title.

articles.headline: Mondo Duplantis equals world record at Athletics Indoor Championships
articles.summary: Swedish pole vaulter Armand 'Mondo' Duplantis successfully defended his world indoor title in Torun, Poland, by clearing a height of 6.25 meters. This performance equals the world record and marks his fourth consecutive global title. Duplantis remains the dominant force in the sport, finishing well ahead of silver medalist Emmanouil Karalis and bronze medalist Ernest John Obiena.

articles.headline: Cow vigilante killed in Agra highway crash during vehicle inspection
articles.summary: A cow vigilante known as Farsa Baba was killed on the Agra-Delhi highway after a truck rammed into a container he had stopped for inspection. While his supporters alleged cattle smugglers were involved, authorities found the vehicle was only carrying soap and shampoo. The accident occurred during dense fog; both the vigilante and the truck driver died from their injuries.

articles.headline: Thar SUV kills auto driver and injures two in Gurugram
articles.summary: A 53-year-old auto-rickshaw driver was killed and two passengers injured after a Mahindra Thar struck their vehicle on the Southern Peripheral Road. The SUV driver abandoned the vehicle and fled the scene. Police have registered an FIR at the Badshahpur station and are searching for the absconding driver. The deceased was the sole breadwinner for his wife and five children.

articles.headline: Five engineering students drown in Godavari river near Bhadrachalam
articles.summary: Five second-year engineering students from SRM and KL Universities drowned in the Godavari river in Eluru district. The group was visiting a temple for Ugadi when they entered a deep stretch of the river to take photographs. One student slipped, and the others were pulled in while trying to rescue him. One member of the group managed to swim to safety.

articles.headline: Police sub-inspector found dead in Telangana after wife's suicide
articles.summary: A 36-year-old sub-inspector was found hanging at his in-laws' house in Karimnagar, four days after his wife allegedly died by suicide. The officer was performing his wife's last rites at the time. His in-laws had previously accused him of being responsible for her death. The couple is survived by two sons, aged 9 and 6. A case has been registered for further investigation.

articles.headline: Wife of man crushed by lift at RG Kar hospital recounts horror
articles.summary: Sonali Banerjee detailed the tragic death of her husband, Arup, who was crushed when a lift malfunctioned at Kolkata’s RG Kar Medical College. The family was trapped in the dark basement before the elevator moved suddenly. While five people have been arrested, the incident has sparked political backlash, with opposition leaders accusing the state government of medical negligence.

articles.headline: Two arrested for smuggling 27 camels in Rajasthan's Kotpuli district
articles.summary: Rajasthan police arrested two individuals for allegedly smuggling 27 camels in a truck intended for sale in other states. The animals were found crammed inside the vehicle during an intercept in Alwar. The accused, from Haryana and Uttar Pradesh, failed to produce any legal permits for transporting the animals. An investigation into the wider smuggling ring is underway.

articles.headline: Kashmiris Donate Valuables to Support Iranians Impacted by Conflict
articles.summary: People across Kashmir are donating cars, jewelry, and cash to support Iran following calls from the Iranian Embassy. The movement, described as a humanitarian response, aims to show solidarity with Iran amid regional aggression. Prominent local figures have pledged a portion of their salaries to the cause.

articles.headline: ULFA(I) Attacks Police Camp in Assam Ahead of Elections
articles.summary: Four personnel were injured when the United Liberation Front of Asom (Independent) attacked a commando camp in Tinsukia. The attack comes as Assam prepares for assembly polls on April 9. Security forces have launched a counter-offensive, while the CM warned that the incident was a desperate attempt to disrupt the democratic process.

articles.headline: Ulfa-I militants attack Assam police camp, four personnel injured
articles.summary: Militants from the Ulfa-I group launched a pre-dawn attack on a police commando camp in Tinsukia, Assam. The attackers used RPGs and automatic weapons, injuring four personnel. The group claimed the attack was a retaliatory strike for recent killings of its members. Security forces have launched 'Operation Bujoni' to locate the insurgents in the nearby forests.

articles.headline: Parents leave infant to beg, child dies of starvation in Bareilly
articles.summary: A one-month-old girl died of starvation in Bareilly after her parents left her at home to go begging in another city. The parents, who are daily wage laborers, were reportedly struggling with extreme poverty and the loss of employment during the pandemic. Police have registered a case after neighbors discovered the infant's body and medical reports confirmed the cause of death.

articles.headline: Delhi themed forest plan for Ridge faces environmentalist opposition
articles.summary: The Delhi government’s plan to create themed forests and 'special vans' in the Central and South Ridge has drawn criticism from activists. Concerns center on potential ecological disturbance to the Capital’s sensitive green zones. Environmentalists argue that such plantations could disrupt the natural ecosystem of one of the city's most critical environmental assets.

articles.headline: Environment Minister warns against unscientific growth in Bhagirathi Eco-Sensitive Zone
articles.summary: Union Minister Bhupender Yadav cautioned that unscientific development in fragile regions like the Bhagirathi Eco-Sensitive Zone risks avoidable disasters. During a meeting in Dehradun, he recommended a precautionary approach guided by evidence-based studies and robust monitoring. The discussion followed activists' alarms regarding ecological impacts of the Char Dham road project.

articles.headline: National Productivity Council to manage environmental audits for green compliance
articles.summary: The commerce ministry has assigned the National Productivity Council (NPC) the responsibility of managing environmental audits and capacity building. The NPC will act as a designated agency to strengthen compliance with environmental laws like the Environment Protection Act. The initiative aims to enhance transparency and promote sustainable industrial growth across the country.

articles.headline: Carcasses of 12 peacocks and seven turtles found in Rajasthan reserve
articles.summary: Wildlife officials discovered 12 dead peacocks and seven turtles in the Ramgarh Vishdhari Tiger Reserve’s buffer zone in Bundi. Preliminary findings suggest the turtles died of suffocation, while the peacocks may have suffered from disease. DCF Arun Kumar stated that exact causes of death will only be confirmed after autopsy reports are finalized by the Indergarh range.

articles.headline: Commentary: Noise pollution from religious festivals damaging public health
articles.summary: Editor Shashi Shekhar argues that the extreme decibel levels during religious festivals like Navratri are causing permanent hearing damage and hypertension. Data shows noise levels in Delhi-NCR regularly exceed 85 decibels, 25 points above WHO limits. He calls for a distinction between religious devotion and auditory aggression, noting that modern boom boxes violate Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita.

articles.headline: Bengaluru suburban rail project fails to save translocated trees
articles.summary: A report by Rail Infrastructure Development Company (Karnataka) reveals that none of the trees translocated for the Bengaluru Suburban Rail Project survived. Out of 279 trees moved between 2022 and 2023, zero survived due to translocation shock and seasonal variations. Environmentalists have criticized the agency for poor planning and failing to maintain the saplings after relocation.

articles.headline: Scientists warn Earth nearing critical 'tipping points' in climate crisis
articles.summary: New research suggests that the Earth may be approaching 16 critical climate tipping points, including the collapse of the Amazon rainforest and the Greenland ice sheet. Crossing these thresholds could lead to a self-sustaining cycle of heating that makes the planet uninhabitable. The study warns that even if emissions stop, some of these processes may have already been set in motion.

articles.headline: National Institute of Open Schooling finances under scrutiny for surplus
articles.summary: A working paper from the National Institute of Public Finance and Policy has raised concerns over NIOS's financial management. The report highlights that the institute generated a surplus of ₹127 crore in 2021-22 while spending twice as much on staff as on student academic needs. Experts question whether funds are being effectively reinvested to improve learning outcomes for dropouts.

articles.headline: Education Ministry cancels UGC-NET June 2024 exam over integrity concerns
articles.summary: The Ministry of Education has cancelled the UGC-NET June 2024 exam following inputs from the National Cyber Crime Threat Analytics Unit suggesting the test's integrity was compromised. The matter has been referred to the CBI for investigation. Over 11 lakh candidates are affected, and a fresh exam date will be announced soon.

articles.headline: PM Internship Scheme PHASE III to Widen Eligibility Rules
articles.summary: The government is easing rules for the PM Internship Scheme to include more sectors and candidates with higher qualifications like MBAs. Firms can now participate without using CSR funds, and the monthly stipend has been raised to ₹6,000. Over 100,000 internships have been offered so far in sectors like semiconductors and renewable energy.

articles.headline: K-pop group BTS returns with massive Seoul concert after military service
articles.summary: Supergroup BTS ended their four-year hiatus with a free comeback concert in Seoul after all seven members completed South Korea’s mandatory military service. The band aims to reclaim its status as a global pop leader. Fans from around the world gathered for the event, which marks the start of a new chapter for the group following their period of individual activities and service.

articles.headline: Hard Rock Cafe closure signals shift in Bengaluru music culture
articles.summary: The likely exit of Hard Rock Cafe from Bengaluru's St Marks Road marks a significant shift in the city's music scene. As the city's economic focus shifts toward EDM and techno, traditional rock venues are finding it harder to sustain operations. Industry insiders suggest that while rock remains popular, the commercial viability of dedicated live music spaces is under threat.

articles.headline: PM Modi chairs CCS meeting to counter West Asia war impact
articles.summary: PM Modi chaired a CCS meeting to review West Asia's impact on India's energy, food, and trade. He directed a 'whole-of-government' approach via a group of ministers to secure coal, fertilizers, pharmaceuticals, and petrochemicals. Efforts focus on diversifying imports, managing supply chains, and preventing black-marketing and hoarding while assessing long-term economic risks.

articles.headline: Narendra Modi Becomes India's Longest-Serving Head of Government
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi has become India's longest-serving head of government, reaching 8,931 days in office and surpassing former Sikkim CM Pawan Kumar Chamling. His tenure includes nearly 13 years as Gujarat CM and his time as PM since 2014. Home Minister Amit Shah hailed the milestone, while officials noted that Modi has served continuously without taking a single day of leave.
